text: The surgeon uses optical entry to access the retromuscular space, visualizing the rectus muscle and initiating insufflation through the first entry trocar. Dissection is performed in the retro rectus areolar plane using a cannula, followed by the placement of ports for robotic operation. The crossover procedure is executed with minimal risk due to the patient's virgin abdomen, characterized by low potential for peritoneal violation and visceral adhesions.

The surgeon applies traction on the tissue to develop the preperitoneal dissection towards the contralateral posterior sheath. Sutures from a prior epigastric hernia repair are visible in the middle of the diastasis.

The surgeon performs a standard crossover dissection, creating a broad space. An incision is made through the linea alba due to a miscalibration of the retro-rectus space. The surgeon discusses the implications of this error on mesh placement and dissection extent.

The surgeon develops the contralateral rectus space and proceeds with the reduction of the umbilical hernia, which contains chronically incarcerated preperitoneal fat. The diastasis is placated to effectively close the defect.

The surgeon reduces the herniated preperitoneal fat and performs limited preperitoneal dissection to separate the peritoneum from the posterior sheath. The diastasis has been addressed, and the retrorectus space is further developed to facilitate broad mesh coverage of the midline.

The surgeon performs anterior fascial closure using a permanent number one V-lock suture, starting below the hernia and diastasis. The suture is applied to placate and reinforce the fascia around the area of the umbilical hernia.

The surgeon anchors the suture with three or four stitches to remove redundancy, followed by sequential tightening to draw the tissues closer together. Multiple sutures are used to placate the diastasis, with sutures run back over themselves to further approximate the tissue. The surgeon takes bites between previous bites to minimize holes in the fascia. A 24 by 30 centimeter mesh is placed, and Tisseel is sprayed over the area to complete the procedure.
